page: Amazon Web Services m6in.16xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor, delivering 64 virtual CPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and includes a 54 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual CPU. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core performance, with the stress-ng multi-core CPU test and PassMark CPU score placing in the top 25%. The processor's cryptographic and extended instruction capabilities are also highly rated, supported by hardware flags such as AVX-512, AES, and SHA_NI. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is in the top 25%, large-block read and write speeds fall into the middle 50%. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in static web serving, database operations, and software compilation, alongside average performance in single-core tasks and large-model LLM inference.

A key feature of the m6in.16xlarge is its high-speed networking, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. This makes it highly suitable for network-intensive workloads, distributed databases, and static web serving. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on external EBS volumes, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy applications. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for enterprise workloads requiring high network throughput and balanced compute-to-memory ratios without the need for dedicated GPU acceleration. Recommended workloads include database operations, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.16xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed, paired with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but offers a high baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput, while single-core performance and large-block memory bandwidth remain average. This hardware profile is highly suitable for network-intensive applications, software compilation, and database workloads that require high network throughput but do not depend on local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for database operations and high-throughput web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps bandwidth, optimized for network-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-bandwidth general-purpose instance featuring sixty-four virtual cores and robust multi-core processing for network-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 64 vCPUs and 256 GB of DDR4 memory
High-speed networking with 100 Gbps baseline bandwidth
Strong multi-core CPU performance and database operations in benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for network-intensive applications, web serving, and databases
